channel.title: AISight — AI Capital Expenditure Tracker
channel.description: Latest news and data on AI infrastructure spending across 38 companies. Updated 3x daily from SEC filings and earnings calls.

item.title: Meta Stock Drops 1.71% as Wall Street Braces for Q1 Earnings Amid $135 Billion AI Spending Blitz
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. shares declined 1.71% in early Monday trading on April 20, 2026, falling $11.76 to $676.79 as investors positioned themselves ahead of the company's first-quarter earnings report later this week and continued to digest the massive capital … [Meta]

item.title: Google is building a four-partner chip supply chain to challenge Nvidia in AI inference
item.description: Summary: Google is building the AI industry’s most diversified custom chip supply chain, with four design partners (Broadcom, MediaTek, Marvell, Intel) and a roadmap stretching from the Ironwood TPU now shipping in the millions to TPU v8 chips at TSMC 2nm in … [TSMC, Intel, Nvidia, Broadcom, Alphabet, ServiceNow]

item.title: Converged Data Center Infrastructure Analysis Report 2026: Market Rising from $6.49 Billion in 2025 to USD 23.89 Billion by 2031, Registering a CAGR of 24.26%
item.description: Key market opportunities in the Global Converged Data Center Infrastructure market include the drive for efficient hybrid cloud environments, demand for AI/ML workloads, and adoption of Infrastructure-as-a-Service models. Challenges include retrofitting legac…

item.title: Korean Stocks Erase War Loss as Chipmakers Rise on AI Resurgence
item.description: South Korean stocks have erased the slide triggered by the Iran war, driven by a rally in chipmakers as escalating US–Iran tensions bring the artificial intelligence trade back into investor focus. [Intel]

item.title: This talent CEO says laid-off tech workers are ignoring a $300K ‘white-collar trade job’ with 81K openings a year
item.description: As AI layoffs threaten the white-collar workforce, the $700 billion data center buildout is minting six-figure technician roles, and employers can't fill them fast enough.
